if you want to make my landing page and website page like how long does it take and also can you make this plan to design this?

User ask design plan for landing page, website. Images show one landing page.

Clarify: "website page" mean landing page, or more pages? Need full site map for more pages.
Assume: HTML, CSS, JavaScript/React for front-end.
Design plan covers technical implementation, not artistic creation.
Estimate depends on scope.

Ask user for clarification on scope.

arguments.reason: Clarify the scope of the user's request regarding "website page" to determine if more than just the landing page needs to be planned.

questions.prompt: You said "landing page and website page" -- what exactly needs designing?

options.label: Just the landing page shown in the images
Landing page + 1 more page (about, services, contact, etc.)
Full site: landing + several internal pages
Not sure yet -- walk me through options

questions.prompt: What kind of output do you want from the plan?

options.label: HTML + CSS code to build the page
React component code (our frontend uses React)
Design spec / mockup only -- I'll code it later
Figma-esque design reference in React Canvas

questions.prompt: What needs designing? Landing page only, or more pages?

options.label: Not sure yet -- show me options
Design spec only -- I code later

content.text: Got it. Full site redesign (landing + several pages), apply to existing codebase with new UX design. Let me understand current frontend setup first.
Let me explore current frontend structure deeper.
Now let me check existing static pages and the current stylesheet to understand the design palette.
Let me check the existing styles to understand the design system.
Now I have a full picture. Let me check the existing panel server to understand how static pages are served.
Now I have a clear picture. Let me present a structured plan.

Here's my assessment of what you have and a proposed design plan.

## Current state

Your site has two layers:

- **Marketing site** -- Next.js SSG, output lives under `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/` (index.html, how-it-works.html, pricing.html, country pages). Served by Python panel server.
- **Auth panel** -- `relocation_jobs/static/index.html` is the SPA shell for the authenticated job board. React + Vite in `frontend/`.

The current homepage is a Next.js SSG export. Its design is functional but dense, and I see room for UX improvement: stronger visual hierarchy, clearer value prop, better information scent for conversion.

## Reference images

The two images you shared show existing landing-page UIs for similar relocation-job products. Both feature hero-search, country/role filters, live result previews, and company cards -- similar content to your current page but laid out differently. Your goal is to improve on that foundation with better UX design.

## Design plan

**Approach:** Create new hand-crafted HTML+CSS files that replace the current Next.js SSG output. This avoids maintaining a Next.js build step for the marketing site and gives full control over layout. The panel SPA (`relocation_jobs/static/index.html`) keeps its layout but gets redesigned to match the new look.

**UX improvements to apply:**

| Issue | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| No clear CTA hierarchy | Hero with single primary action ("Search roles"), secondary nav less prominent |
| Feature cards lack progression | 3-step journey: preview -> track -> tailor |
| No social proof / trust | Add "Used by X engineers" counter, country badges with live counts |
| Board preview static | Replace snippet with interactive-looking preview that links to sign-in |
| Mobile nav hidden behind hamburger only | Bottom-anchored sticky nav on mobile |
| Country page layout inconsistent | Uniform card layout across all country pages |
| Empty/loading states on board | Animated skeleton, clear empty-state messaging |
| Color contrast on some pills | WCAG AA on all status badges |

**Pages to redesign** (keeping URL structure identical):

1. **Homepage** (`relocation_jobs/static/homepage/index.html`) -- Hero with search, stats bar, 3-step feature section, live board preview, CTA, footer
2. **How it works** (`relocation_jobs/static/homepage/how-it-works.html`) -- Step-through layout with illustrations
3. **Pricing** (`relocation_jobs/static/homepage/pricing.html`) -- Tier cards, feature comparison
4. **Country pages** (Germany, Netherlands, UK, Portugal) -- Consistent template with localized hero, company grid, country stats
5. **404** (`relocation_jobs/static/homepage/404.html`) -- Branded 404 with search fallback
6. **Panel app shell** (`relocation_jobs/static/index.html`) -- Align with new design system, same structure
7. **Company page** (`relocation_jobs/static/company.html`) -- Light touch, header alignment

**Design system** -- Extend existing CSS custom properties in `relocation_jobs/static/styles.css`. Current palette (dark theme, Inter font, gradient accent) stays but gets:
- New `--text-section-title`, `--text-fluid-hero` sizes
- New surface-card interactive states
- Hero section glow + entry animations
- Pill-control unified component style
- `reveal-card` scroll-trigger animation
- Bottom sticky mobile nav

**Files to create/modify:**

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `relocation_jobs/static/styles.css` | Add new section (mark homepage styles), append without breaking existing styles |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/index.html` | Rewrite with new layout |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/how-it-works.html` | Rewrite |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/pricing.html` | Rewrite |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/relocation-jobs-germany.html` | Rewrite template |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/relocation-jobs-netherlands.html` | Rewrite template |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/relocation-jobs-uk.html` | Rewrite template |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/relocation-jobs-portugal.html` | Rewrite template |
| `relocation_jobs/static/homepage/404.html` | Rewrite |
| `relocation_jobs/static/index.html` | Update header, footer, color tokens to match new system |
| `relocation_jobs/static/company.html` | Update header |
| `relocation_jobs/static/apply.html` | Update header |
| `relocation_jobs/static/admin.html` | Update header |
| `relocation_jobs/static/public.html` | Update header |

**Time estimate:** ~6-8 hours for full implementation spread across 2-3 sessions:

- Session 1 (2-3h): Design system + homepage
- Session 2 (2-3h): Inner pages + country templates
- Session 3 (1-2h): Panel alignment + review

**Dependencies:** None. All changes are self-contained HTML+CSS. No build step needed.

Does this plan look right? Any pages I missed or any specific UX pattern you want prioritised?

<timestamp>Saturday, Jul 18, 2026, 12:12 AM (UTC+4)</timestamp>

Generating the remaining pages from a shared template via a short script:

arguments.command:
python3 << 'PY' from pathlib import Path ROOT = Path("/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/homepage") HEAD = ''' {title}
''' HEADER = '''
Relocation Jobs Visa-friendly roles abroad
  • How it works
  • Pricing
  • What you get
  • See the board
Sign in RJ
  • How it works
  • Pricing
  • What you get
  • See the board
  • Sign in
''' FOOTER = '''

Relocation Jobs

  • Board
  • How it works
  • Pricing
  • Jobs in Germany
  • Jobs in Netherlands
  • Jobs in UK
  • Jobs in Portugal
  • Contributing
  • Source
''' def page(meta, main_html): return ( HEAD.format(**meta) + HEADER + main_html + FOOTER ) # how-it-works (ROOT / "how-it-works.html").write_text(page({ "title": "How It Works | Relocation Jobs", "description": "Relocation Jobs tracks visa-friendly software engineering roles across Europe. Search the public preview, then sign in to track applications and tailor your CV per job.", "robots": "index, follow", "og_title": "How It Works | Relocation Jobs", "og_description": "Search the public preview, sign in to track applications and manage your job search across Germany, Netherlands, UK, and Portugal.", "og_url": "https://kuchup.com/how-it-works", }, '''

How It Works

Relocation Jobs is a curated search tool for software engineers looking for visa-sponsored roles in Europe. We scrape company career pages every 6 hours so you see openings as they appear — not listings syndicated from aggregators.

1. Browse the public preview

See which countries and companies are actively hiring. The homepage shows a live overview of supported countries and sampled company preview cards — no sign-in required.

2. Sign in to unlock tracking

Once you sign in, the full board is available: company-level tracking, per-user state for every role, apply/reject/not-for-me buckets, and a company workspace to store CVs and application documents.

3. Tailor your CV per job

Use the built-in reframe pipeline to align your CV with specific job descriptions. Project masters, cover letters, and LaTeX exports — all inside the workspace.

4. Stay current

The catalog refreshes automatically every 6 hours. New roles from company career pages appear in the board the same day. No stale syndicated listings.

Sign in to start tracking
'''), encoding="utf-8") print("how-it-works.html") # pricing (ROOT / "pricing.html").write_text(page({ "title": "Pricing | Relocation Jobs", "description": "Plans and packages for Relocation Jobs — free preview available; full board access and workspace tools coming soon.", "robots": "index, follow", "og_title": "Pricing | Relocation Jobs", "og_description": "Free public preview available. Paid plans for full board access, company workspaces, and CV reframe tools coming soon.", "og_url": "https://kuchup.com/pricing", }, '''

Plans & Pricing

Relocation Jobs is in active development. The public preview is free for everyone. Paid packages for full board access, company workspace tools, and CV reframe pipeline are being designed.

Free Preview

$0

  • Public catalog overview
  • Company preview cards
  • Country-level stats
  • Search public preview
Browse preview

Full Access

Coming soon

  • Full company board
  • Per-user apply/reject tracking
  • Company workspace + documents
  • Tailored CV reframe pipeline
  • Cover letter generation
Sign in for early access

Pricing details will be announced when the full access plan launches. Current users get grandfathered access.

'''), encoding="utf-8") print("pricing.html") # 404 (ROOT / "404.html").write_text(page({ "title": "Page not found | Relocation Jobs", "description": "This page could not be found.", "robots": "noindex", "og_title": "Page not found | Relocation Jobs", "og_description": "This page could not be found.", "og_url": "https://kuchup.com", }, '''

404

Page not found

That URL is not part of the public site. Try the homepage or open the job board.

Go home Open board
'''), encoding="utf-8") print("404.html") countries = [ { "slug": "relocation-jobs-germany", "name": "Germany", "title": "Visa-Sponsored Software Jobs in Germany | Relocation Jobs", "description": "Find software engineering jobs in Germany with visa sponsorship. Browse companies hiring in Berlin, Munich, and beyond.", "og_description": "Software engineering roles in Germany with visa sponsorship. Browse Berlin, Munich, and remote-friendly companies.", "intro": "Germany is one of Europe's strongest markets for visa-sponsored tech roles. With the EU Blue Card, a growing startup scene in Berlin, and established tech hubs in Munich and Hamburg, companies actively recruit software engineers from abroad.", "hubs": [ ("Berlin", "Startup capital, strong in fintech, e-commerce, and mobility."), ("Munich", "Enterprise software, automotive tech, and SaaS."), ("Hamburg", "E-commerce, logistics, and media technology."), ("Cologne / Dusseldorf", "Media, telecom, and insurance tech."), ], "visa": "The EU Blue Card is the most common route for skilled software engineers. Germany also offers the Chancenkarte (Opportunity Card) for job seekers and the IT specialist visa for experienced professionals without a formal degree. Most employers on Relocation Jobs list visa sponsorship in their openings.", "panel": "germany", }, { "slug": "relocation-jobs-netherlands", "name": "Netherlands", "title": "Visa-Sponsored Software Jobs in Netherlands | Relocation Jobs", "description": "Find software engineering jobs in the Netherlands with visa sponsorship. Browse companies hiring in Amsterdam, Rotterdam, and beyond.", "og_description": "Software engineering roles in the Netherlands with visa sponsorship. Browse Amsterdam, Rotterdam, and remote-friendly companies.", "intro": "The Netherlands is a top destination for international engineers — English-friendly workplaces, a strong 30% ruling tax benefit, and a dense startup and scale-up scene around Amsterdam and Eindhoven.", "hubs": [ ("Amsterdam", "Fintech, marketplaces, and product-led SaaS."), ("Eindhoven", "Hardware, deep tech, and semiconductor-adjacent roles."), ("Rotterdam", "Logistics tech, energy, and industrial software."), ("Utrecht", "Healthtech, public-sector digital, and scale-ups."), ], "visa": "The Highly Skilled Migrant (kennismigrant) permit is the standard route. Many employers are recognized sponsors and can hire from abroad. The 30% ruling can make relocation financially attractive for the first years.", "panel": "netherlands", }, { "slug": "relocation-jobs-uk", "name": "UK", "title": "Visa-Sponsored Software Jobs in UK | Relocation Jobs", "description": "Find software engineering jobs in the UK with visa sponsorship. Browse companies hiring in London and beyond.", "og_description": "Software engineering roles in the UK with visa sponsorship. Browse London and remote-friendly companies.", "intro": "The UK remains a major tech market for sponsored roles. London dominates product and fintech hiring; many scale-ups and enterprises are licensed sponsors under the Skilled Worker visa route.", "hubs": [ ("London", "Fintech, SaaS, and large product engineering orgs."), ("Manchester", "Growing tech scene and remote-friendly employers."), ("Edinburgh", "Fintech and enterprise software."), ("Bristol", "Creative tech, aerospace-adjacent software, and startups."), ], "visa": "Skilled Worker visas require a licensed sponsor. On Relocation Jobs, look for roles that explicitly mention sponsorship or relocation support — not every UK listing can sponsor.", "panel": "uk", }, { "slug": "relocation-jobs-portugal", "name": "Portugal", "title": "Visa-Sponsored Software Jobs in Portugal | Relocation Jobs", "description": "Find software engineering jobs in Portugal with visa sponsorship. Browse companies hiring in Lisbon, Porto, and beyond.", "og_description": "Software engineering roles in Portugal with visa sponsorship. Browse Lisbon, Porto, and remote-friendly companies.", "intro": "Portugal has become a popular base for engineers relocating to Europe — competitive cost of living, strong Lisbon and Porto tech scenes, and employers open to international hiring.", "hubs": [ ("Lisbon", "Product startups, marketplaces, and nearshore engineering hubs."), ("Porto", "Software services, product teams, and growing scale-ups."), ("Remote / hybrid", "Many teams hire internationally with Portugal as the employment base."), ], "visa": "Tech employers typically use work visas or EU Blue Card pathways for non-EU hires. Confirm sponsorship and employment location on each posting — remote-from-Portugal setups vary by company.", "panel": "portugal", }, ] for c in countries: hubs_html = "".join( f'
  • {h} — {d}
    • ' for h, d in c["hubs"] ) main = f'''

    Visa-Friendly Software Jobs in {c["name"]}

    {c["intro"]}

    Key tech hubs

      {hubs_html}

    Visa pathways

    {c["visa"]}

    What you'll find on Relocation Jobs

    • Companies actively hiring software engineers in {c["name"]}
    • Visa-friendly roles flagged per job opening
    • Fresh data scraped every 6 hours from career pages
    • Track applications and manage your search in one board
    Browse {c["name"]} companies
    ''' (ROOT / f"{c['slug']}.html").write_text(page({ "title": c["title"], "description": c["description"], "robots": "index, follow", "og_title": c["title"], "og_description": c["og_description"], "og_url": f"https://kuchup.com/{c['slug']}", }, main), encoding="utf-8") print(c["slug"] + ".html") print("done") PY

todos.content: Wire panel to honor ?country= (and optional ?q=) from marketing CTAs
Rebuild homepage with shared shell + working search forms

content.text:
Continued with the functional wiring:

1. **Search forms work** — Homepage “Search roles” submits to `/panel?country=…&q=…`
2. **Panel reads URL params** — `?country=` sets the country filter; `?q=` fills the search box ([`data.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/data.js))
3. **Branded 404** — Unknown URLs now serve the marketing 404 page ([`server.py`](relocation_jobs/web/server.py))
4. **Homepage cleaned up** — Same shared shell as the other marketing pages

Hard-refresh [http://127.0.0.1:5051/](http://127.0.0.1:5051/) and try Search with a country selected.
